item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why comment at all if you can't be bothered reading it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Stopped reading at referencing flour. Figured it was cocaine and moved on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really dense, and it reminds me of another skip about alternate minds catching a ride in people's bodies, but the hole imagery alone was worth an upvote.

…Until I got to the exploration log. It gives this a lot more depth, no pun intended, but the dialogue isn't entirely on point. The MTF sound comical in places, and that -1 is all but doing villainous monologuing. My good graces were worn by the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hesitant upvote. Everything here is great; the writing is on-point, and the story isn't half bad, but my major concern lies in the fact that this is just cocaine that makes zombies doesn't do it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was interested at first, but having everything turn out to be the borg with designer drugs killed that, especially the "we have already won, you will be assimilated" dialogue from the -1. On a semi-related note, I didn't get a "trained professional" vibe from the logs with the MTF.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You've always been hard to please psuuul

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Long sections of this come across as entirely overblown. This is particularly true of the dialogue sections - the first interview feels like every possible cliched phrase was worked into it, with neither party sounding like a realistic human, and the third is heavily committed to a very tired trope. The mission log could be cut down to about a third of its current length without losing any important information, and it would probably gain in realism and tension - too much time is spent on preliminaries that are distracting and don't serve to create a connection to the MTF characters.

In addition, the "bait and switch" narrative (replacing the drug with the murderous collective consciousness) means that the Containment Procedures are insufficient - they don't say anything about containing the -1 instances, and the high likelihood of outbreak makes the current procedures seem lax. The consciousness itself isn't given any substantive characterisation (leaving it in the "generic evil assimilation" category), meaning the story ends before it can get interesting.

Overall, this is a very large amount of text to tell a "substance that makes you a murderous zombie" story (and zombies is the right analogy, on the basis of loss of agency and compulsion to spread the effects). That's not new ground, and the work that's been put into this isn't really enlivening the idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sure? Except replace zombie with collective consciousness and virus with designer drugs.

There is no reanimation of the dead, no cannibalism (hemotaphagy is more vampiric than zombie-like.)

Plus there us the whole "remove the human half of your brain and attach it to a neural network" bit but okay!

Thats just, like, your opinion, man

Also, there's a big metaphor for addiction here- giving in, letting drugs control your life. One day you might look in the mirror and not recognize who you are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is your everyday zombie virus, in my opinion, even more so than 008. I cannot, in good intention, upvote. Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Lovecraft's Mi-Go pushing designer drugs?!

This was delightfully disturbing. Just enough gorn to paint the picture (and the walls and floor), but not enough to be gratuitous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a rewrite of one that I wasn't really happy with, I removed most of the jargon (While leaving a little), and amped up the horror

Thanks to folks on the IRC for help

Picture was made by me

Don't do drugs kids.
